# Adaptive MCP

> Turn MCP usage into learned metadata, so clients adapt instead of guessing.
> A learning layer over MCP primitives (tools, resources) — it does not replace
> MCP, redefine tools, or introduce new protocol abstractions.

Adaptive MCP observes how MCP tools are actually used and attaches learned
metadata to existing primitives. All metadata lives in a SQLite store (the
single source of truth); the `tools-metadata.yaml` view is a derived projection,
recomputed whenever metadata changes. The server governs a narrow, server-governed
resource (`dev.adaptivemcp/tools-metadata`); the client learning machinery reads
it, adapts, and reports observations back, degrading gracefully on any host that
ignores it.

## Docs

- Getting Started: https://adaptivemcp.dev/guide/getting-started
- Architecture: https://adaptivemcp.dev/guide/architecture
- The tools-metadata Extension: https://adaptivemcp.dev/guide/extension
- Packages: https://adaptivemcp.dev/packages
- Examples: https://adaptivemcp.dev/examples

## Packages

- @adaptivemcp/spec — Extension identifiers, event schemas, shared types
- @adaptivemcp/memory — SQLite SSOT store (MemoryStore) over node:sqlite
- @adaptivemcp/telemetry — Tool execution events and observability
- @adaptivemcp/evaluation — Outcome scoring and feedback loops
- @adaptivemcp/extension — Derives the YAML view and serves the MCP resource

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/kemalelmizan/adaptive-mcp
- npm organization: https://www.npmjs.com/org/adaptivemcp
